patterns.name: Anthropic API Key
patterns.remediation: Rotate the API key immediately at console.anthropic.com. Replace with environment variable ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

patterns.name: OpenAI API Key
patterns.remediation: Rotate the API key at platform.openai.com. Replace with environment variable OPENAI_API_KEY.

patterns.name: AWS Access Key ID
patterns.remediation: Rotate the AWS access key immediately in IAM console. Use IAM roles or environment variables.

patterns.name: AWS Secret Access Key
patterns.description: AWS secret access key in assignment
patterns.remediation: Rotate the secret key in IAM. Use environment variables or AWS Secrets Manager.

patterns.name: Google API Key
patterns.remediation: Restrict or delete the key in Google Cloud Console. Use service accounts instead.

patterns.name: Google OAuth Client ID
patterns.remediation: Regenerate OAuth credentials in Google Cloud Console. Store in environment variables.

patterns.name: GitHub Personal Access Token
patterns.description: GitHub personal access token or app token detected
patterns.remediation: Revoke the token at github.com/settings/tokens. Use fine-grained tokens with minimal scope.

patterns.name: GitHub OAuth Token
patterns.remediation: Revoke the OAuth token. Regenerate via OAuth flow.

patterns.name: Slack Token
patterns.description: Slack bot, user, or app token detected
patterns.remediation: Revoke the token in Slack app settings. Regenerate and store as environment variable.

patterns.name: Stripe Secret Key
patterns.description: Stripe live secret key detected — grants full API access
patterns.remediation: Roll the key in Stripe Dashboard immediately. Use restricted keys with minimal permissions.

patterns.name: Stripe Publishable Key (Live)
patterns.description: Stripe live publishable key — less sensitive but should not be in server code
patterns.remediation: Move to frontend-only code or environment variable.

patterns.name: Twilio API Key
patterns.remediation: Revoke the key in Twilio Console. Use environment variable TWILIO_API_KEY.

patterns.name: SendGrid API Key
patterns.remediation: Revoke the key in SendGrid Settings. Use environment variable SENDGRID_API_KEY.

patterns.name: Mailgun API Key
patterns.remediation: Rotate in Mailgun dashboard. Use environment variable MAILGUN_API_KEY.

patterns.name: Resend API Key
patterns.remediation: Revoke in Resend dashboard. Use environment variable RESEND_API_KEY.

patterns.name: Azure Storage/Service Bus Key
patterns.description: Azure storage or service bus shared key detected
patterns.remediation: Rotate key in Azure Portal. Use Managed Identity or Key Vault.

patterns.name: JWT Secret
patterns.remediation: Move to environment variable. Use asymmetric keys (RS256) instead of shared secrets.

patterns.name: Password Assignment
patterns.remediation: Remove hardcoded password. Use environment variable or secrets manager.

patterns.name: Secret Assignment
patterns.remediation: Remove hardcoded secret. Use environment variable or secrets manager.

patterns.name: Token Assignment
patterns.remediation: Remove hardcoded token. Use environment variable or OAuth flow.

patterns.name: API Key Assignment
patterns.description: Hardcoded API key in assignment statement
patterns.remediation: Remove hardcoded key. Use environment variable.

patterns.name: Connection String
patterns.description: Database connection string with potential embedded credentials
patterns.remediation: Use environment variable for connection strings. Never embed credentials in code.

patterns.name: Private Key Header
patterns.description: Private key content embedded in file
patterns.remediation: Remove private key from repo immediately. Store in secure key management system. If exposed, regenerate the key pair.

patterns.name: Bearer Token in Code
patterns.description: Bearer authentication token in code or config
patterns.remediation: Remove hardcoded bearer token. Use environment variable or token refresh mechanism.

patterns.name: Basic Auth Header
patterns.description: Basic authentication header with encoded credentials
patterns.remediation: Remove hardcoded auth header. Use environment variables for credentials.

patterns.name: URL with Embedded Credentials
patterns.remediation: Remove credentials from URL. Use separate auth configuration.
